---
title: "In Silico"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# RADinitio

<https://catchenlab.life.illinois.edu/radinitio/>

The following scripts are done on the example of the *Hypericum pulchrum* reference genome.

## 1 Population Simulation

Path: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/radinitio_pop_10_5.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_pop   
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=10G
#SBATCH --time=120:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_pop_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_pop_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Population Simulation at $(date) ==="

mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/

# Run population simulation only - FIXED genome path
radinitio --make-population \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --n-pops 10 \
   --n-seq-indv 5

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Population simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Check outputs
   echo "=== Output files ==="
   ls -lh /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/

   # Check for VCF file
   VCF_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz"
   if [ -f "$VCF_FILE" ]; then
       echo "✓ VCF file created: $VCF_FILE"
       echo "  Number of variants: $(zcat $VCF_FILE 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^#' | wc -l || echo 'unable to count')"
   else
       echo "✗ WARNING: VCF file not found!"
       echo "  Contents of population directory:"
       find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ -type f -name "*.vcf*" 2>/dev/null || echo "  No VCF files found"
   fi

   # Check popmap
   POPMAP_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv"
   if [ -f "$POPMAP_FILE" ]; then
       echo "✓ Popmap file created: $POPMAP_FILE"
       echo "  Number of individuals: $(wc -l < $POPMAP_FILE)"
       echo "  First 5 lines:"
       head -5 "$POPMAP_FILE"
   else
       echo "✗ WARNING: Popmap file not found!"
   fi
else
   echo "Population simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Population Simulation Complete ==="
```

## 2 Library Simulation

Path: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/radinitio_lib_10_5.sh

This is with the enzyme KpnI

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_lib
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=400G
#SBATCH --time=120:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Library Simulation at $(date) ==="

# Check if population simulation exists
if [ ! -d "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/msprime_vcfs/" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: msprime_vcfs directory not found!"
   echo "Please run the population simulation first."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: Population VCF file (ri_master.vcf.gz) not found!"
   echo "Population simulation may not have completed successfully."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: popmap.tsv file not found!"
   echo "The population map file is required for the simulation."
   exit 1
fi

# Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation

# Run library simulation
radinitio --make-library-seq \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/ \
   --make-pop-sim-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --library-type sdRAD \
   --enz KpnI \
   --coverage 20 \
   --read-length 150 \
   --insert-mean 350 \
   --insert-stdev 37 \
   --lib-bar1-len 6 \
   --read-out-fmt fastq

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Library simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Find and display barcode file
   BARCODE_FILE=$(find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/ -name "*barcode*.tsv" -o -name "*barcode*.txt" 2>/dev/null | head -1)

   if [ -n "$BARCODE_FILE" ]; then
       echo ""
       echo "=== BARCODE FILE ==="
       echo "Location: $BARCODE_FILE"
       echo "Total barcodes: $(wc -l < $BARCODE_FILE)"
       echo ""
       echo "First 10 barcodes:"
       head -10 "$BARCODE_FILE"

       # Copy to convenient location
       cp "$BARCODE_FILE" /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv
       echo ""
       echo "✓ Barcode file copied to: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_1/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv"
   fi
else
   echo "Library simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Library Simulation Complete ==="
```

Path: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/radinitio_lib_10_1_PstI.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_lib
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=400G
#SBATCH --time=120:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Library Simulation at $(date) ==="

mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5

# Check if population simulation exists
if [ ! -d "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/msprime_vcfs/" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: msprime_vcfs directory not found!"
   echo "Please run the population simulation first."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: Population VCF file (ri_master.vcf.gz) not found!"
   echo "Population simulation may not have completed successfully."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: popmap.tsv file not found!"
   echo "The population map file is required for the simulation."
   exit 1
fi

# Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/PstI/simulation

# Run library simulation
radinitio --make-library-seq \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/PstI/simulation/ \
   --make-pop-sim-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --library-type sdRAD \
   --enz PstI \
   --coverage 20 \
   --read-length 150 \
   --insert-mean 350 \
   --insert-stdev 37 \
   --lib-bar1-len 6 \
   --read-out-fmt fastq

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Library simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Find and display barcode file
   BARCODE_FILE=$(find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/PstI/simulation/ -name "*barcode*.tsv" -o -name "*barcode*.txt" 2>/dev/null | head -1)

   if [ -n "$BARCODE_FILE" ]; then
       echo ""
       echo "=== BARCODE FILE ==="
       echo "Location: $BARCODE_FILE"
       echo "Total barcodes: $(wc -l < $BARCODE_FILE)"
       echo ""
       echo "First 10 barcodes:"
       head -10 "$BARCODE_FILE"

       # Copy to convenient location
       cp "$BARCODE_FILE" /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/PstI/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv
       echo ""
       echo "✓ Barcode file copied to: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/PstI/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv"
   fi
else
   echo "Library simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Library Simulation Complete ==="
```

## 3 Mapping

The mapping was done for every enzyme for 10_5 with the reference genome of *Hypericum pulchrum*

Script: mapping_radinitio_KpnI.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=map_radinitio_KpnI
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=32gb
#SBATCH --array=1-50
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

# Directories
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/rad_reads"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping"
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"

# Create output directory if needed
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Use the random 100 sample list
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INDIR}/sample_list_10_5_new.txt)

FQ1=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.1.fq.gz
FQ2=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.2.fq.gz
BAM=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

echo "Mapping sample: $SAMPLE"
echo "FQ1: $FQ1"
echo "FQ2: $FQ2"
echo "Output BAM: $BAM"

module purge
module load bio/bwa-mem2/2.2.1-intel-compilers-2023.1.0

bwa-mem2 mem -t 6 $REF $FQ1 $FQ2 > ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam


module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.19.2-GCC-13.2.0   # Pick a SAMtools version compatible with GCC

samtools view -bS ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam | samtools sort -o $BAM -
samtools index $BAM

rm ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam
```

## 4 Deduplication of Mapped Reads

After the mapping, the deduplication was done. Again for every enzyme from 10_5

Script: dedup_radinitio_KpnI.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=dedup_radinitio
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --array=1-50
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j_%a.err

module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.18-GCC-12.3.0

INLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/rad_reads/sample_list_10_5_new.txt"
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup"

mkdir -p $OUTDIR

# Get sample name
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INLIST})

echo "Processing $SAMPLE"

# Input BAM
INBAM=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

# Intermediate + final BAMs
NAME_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_name_sorted.bam
FIXMATE=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate.bam
FIXMATE_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate_sorted.bam
DEDUP=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_sorted_dedup.bam
STATS=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_duplicates_report.txt

# Step 1: sort by name
samtools sort -n -@ 6 -O bam -o $NAME_SORT $INBAM

# Step 2: fixmate
samtools fixmate -m -O bam $NAME_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $NAME_SORT

# Step 3: coordinate sort
samtools sort -@ 6 -O bam -o $FIXMATE_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $FIXMATE

# Step 4: mark duplicates (remove them with -r, keep stats with -s)
samtools markdup -r -s -@ 6 $FIXMATE_SORT $DEDUP
rm -f $FIXMATE_SORT

# Step 5: stats summary
samtools flagstat $DEDUP > $STATS

samtools index $DEDUP

echo "Finished $SAMPLE"
```

## 5 (Multi)BamQC

This was done for every enzyme from 10_5

Script: multibamqc_KpnI.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=multibamqc_KpnI
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

BAM_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup"
BAMQC_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/bamqc/"
MULTIBAMQC_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/multibamqc"

mkdir -p "$BAMQC_DIR" "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR"

# Set Java options for headless mode
export JAVA_OPTS="-Djava.awt.headless=true"

# Run Qualimap BAMQC for each sorted BAM file
for BAM_FILE in "$BAM_DIR"/*_sorted_dedup.bam; do
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "$BAM_FILE" _sorted_dedup.bam)
QUALIMAP_OUTPUT_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/$SAMPLE_NAME"

# Define output and error log files for each sample INSIDE the loop
SBATCH_OUTPUT_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/${SAMPLE_NAME}_bamqc.out"
SBATCH_ERROR_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/${SAMPLE_NAME}_bamqc.err"

echo "Running Qualimap BAMQC for $SAMPLE_NAME"
qualimap bamqc \
-bam "$BAM_FILE" \
-outdir "$QUALIMAP_OUTPUT_DIR" \
-outformat HTML \
-outfile "${SAMPLE_NAME}_report" \
-nw 4 \
> "$SBATCH_OUTPUT_FILE" 2> "$SBATCH_ERROR_FILE"
done

# Prepare input groups file for multi-bamqc
INPUT_GROUPS_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/input_groups.txt"
rm -f "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"

echo "Creating input groups file for Multi-BAMQC"
for BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR in "$BAMQC_DIR"/*; do
if [ -d "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR" ] && [ -f "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR/genome_results.txt" ]; then
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR")
echo -e "${SAMPLE_NAME}\t${BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR}\t1" >> "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"
fi
done

# Run Qualimap Multi-BAMQC
echo "Running Qualimap Multi-BAMQC"
qualimap multi-bamqc \
-d "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE" \
-outdir "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR" \
-outformat PDF:HTML \
-nw 4

echo "Qualimap analysis complete. Reports saved in $MULTIBAMQC_DIR."
```

## 6 VCF Calling

Make the bam_file_paths_sorted.txt from the files in the dedup directory

``` bash
find "$(pwd)" -maxdepth 1 -name "*_sorted_dedup.bam" | sort > bam_file_paths_sorted.txt
```

Script: vcf_calling_radinitio_KpnI.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash

#SBATCH --job-name=vcf_calling_radinitio_KpnI
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

module purge
module load bio/BCFtools/1.19-GCC-13.2.0

# Set paths
# Referencegenome
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"
# List with all the paths to the .sorted.bam files
BAMLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted.txt"
# Defines Output where to put the vcf
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"
OUT="${OUTDIR}/KpnI.vcf.gz"

mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Print job info to log file
echo "Starting variant calling with bcftools" > $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Reference Genome: $REF" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "BAM list: $BAMLIST" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Output VCF: $OUT" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log echo "Start time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

bcftools mpileup \
-f $REF \
-b $BAMLIST \
-q 20 \
-Q 30 \
-a AD,DP,SP \
-Ou \
| bcftools call \
-m -v \
-a GQ,GP \
-Oz \
-o $OUT

echo "End time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
```

## 7 Pre-Visualisation

Script: pre_vis_KpnI.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=vcftools_stats
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --time=01:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

# Load module
module purge
module load bio/VCFtools/0.1.16-GCC-11.3.0

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/KpnI.vcf.gz"
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcftools"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

echo "Starting VCFtools statistics"
echo "VCF: $SET_VCF"
echo "Output directory: $OUT"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT
```

## 8 Visualisation and Patchwork Plots

``` r
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lqual"),
c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.ldepth.mean"),
c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lmiss"),
c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_raw <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.frq"),
c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))
parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))
var_freq <- var_freq_raw %>%
mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
maf = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.idepth"),
c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.imiss"),
c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.het"),
c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F")) # F ~ inbreeding coefficient

write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Patchwork Plot

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 800)) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 75)) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


#save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_raw_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)
```

## 9 Summary TSV of the Raw VCF Stats

``` r
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for raw VCF")
```

## 10 Filtering

### Filtering by Site

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_site1_KpnI
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

vcftools    --gzvcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/KpnI.vcf.gz \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --mac 3 \
    --min-alleles 2 \
    --max-alleles 2 \
    --min-meanDP 5 \
    --max-meanDP 40 \
    --minDP 5 \
    --minQ 30 \
    --max-missing 0.8 \
    --out /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80 \
    --recode \
    --remove-indels

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 
```

### Visualisation of Filtered by Site VCF Stats

``` r
# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS
# R Script
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site1.csv"))  

# PATCHWORK PLOT OF VARIOUS VCF FILTERED STATS

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site1.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site1.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site1.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site1.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site1.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site1.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site1.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) +  
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


# Save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/vcf_filt_site1_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)

# SUMMARY TABLE OF FILTERED VCF STATS
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site1.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site1.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site1.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site1.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site1.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site1.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site1.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/vcf_filt_site1_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/vcf_filt_site1_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for filtered by site VCF")
```

As the individuals have no high missingness, there was no filtering by individual needed.

## 11 PCA

``` bash
bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf | awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' | awk -F'.sorted.bam' '{print $1}' | awk -F'_' '{print $2 "_" $1 "_" $3}' > new_sample_names.txt

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf | awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' | awk -F'.sorted.bam' '{print $1}' | awk -F'_' '{print $2 "_" $1 "_" $4}' > new_sample_names.txt

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf > old_sample_names.txt

paste old_sample_names.txt new_sample_names.txt > rename_samples.txt

bcftools reheader -s rename_samples.txt minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf -o minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_shortnames.vcf

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_shortnames.vcf

plink --vcf minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_shortnames.vcf --double-id --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80

plink --bfile minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80 --pca --allow-extra-chr --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_pca
```

``` r
install.packages("readr")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("ggrepel")
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Read eigenvectors ---
pca <- read_table(
"/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_pca.eigenvec",
col_names = FALSE
)

colnames(pca) <- c(
  "FID",
  "IID",
  paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-2))
)

# --- Create clean sample IDs matching popmap.tsv ---
pca$sample <- paste0(
  "msp_",
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID)
)

# --- Create short labels like 00_msp ---
pca$label <- paste0(
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID),
  "_msp"
)

# --- Read population assignments ---
colnames(pca) <- c(
  "FID",
  "IID",
  paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-2))
)

# --- Create clean sample IDs matching popmap.tsv ---
pca$sample <- paste0(
  "msp_",
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID)
)

# --- Create short labels like 00_msp ---
pca$label <- paste0(
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID),
  "_msp"
)

popmap <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv",
  col_names = c("sample", "group")
)

# --- Merge into PCA table ---
pca <- left_join(pca, popmap, by = "sample")

# --- Colors for groups ---
library(scales)

group_levels <- unique(pca$group)

group_colors <- setNames(
  hue_pal()(length(group_levels)),
  group_levels
)

pca_plot <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
theme_bw() +
labs(title = "PCA PC1-PC2")

# --- Save to file directly ---
ggsave(
filename = "PCA_PC1_PC2.pdf", # file name
plot = pca_plot,
width = 6, # width in inches
height = 5, # height in inches
dpi = 300 # resolution
)

# --- Read eigenvalues (correct file!) ---
eigval <- scan(
"/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_pca.eigenval"
)


# --- Compute percentage variance explained ---
percent <- eigval / sum(eigval) * 100

# --- PCA plot ---
pca1_2 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
geom_text_repel(
aes(label = label),
size = 3,
max.overlaps = Inf
) +
scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
theme_bw() +
labs(
title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 1-2",
x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent[1], 2), "%)"),
y = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent[2], 2), "%)")
)

pca1_3 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC3, color = group)) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
geom_text_repel(
aes(label = label),
size = 3,
max.overlaps = Inf
) +
scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
theme_bw() +
labs(
title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 1-3",
x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent[1], 2), "%)"),
y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent[3], 2), "%)")
)

pca2_3 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC2, PC3, color = group)) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
geom_text_repel(
aes(label = label),
size = 3,
max.overlaps = Inf
) +
scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
theme_bw() +
labs(
title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 2-3",
x = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent[2], 2), "%)"),
y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent[3], 2), "%)")
)

#save plots as pdf files
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/pca1_2_hypericumpulchrum.pdf", pca1_2, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/pca1_3_hypericumpulchrum.pdf", pca1_3, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/pca2_3_hypericumpulchrum.pdf", pca2_3, width = 8, height = 6)
```

## 12 Counting SNPs

``` bash
bcftools view -H name_of_last_vcf | wc -l
```
